DataFlow Systems

Democratizing real-time data intelligence for modern enterprises

Now Hiring: Staff Data Engineer

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid) · Full-time · $240,000 - $310,000 USD + equity (0.15-0.35%)

W

e are seeking a Staff Data Engineer with 8-12 years of experience to lead the evolution of our core data

infrastructure platform. In this role, you will architect and own large-scale distributed data systems, mentor

engineering teams, and drive technical strategy across the organization. You'll work on mission-critical systems

handling petabyte-scale workloads, partner with product and customer teams on architectural decisions, and

establish best practices that define our engineering culture.

A B O U T U S

DataFlow Systems is a Series B data infrastructure company helping enterprises build scalable, real-time data

pipelines. Founded in 2020, we've grown to serve 150+ mid-market and enterprise clients across fintech, retail,

and healthcare, processing 10+ petabytes of data monthly. Our platform abstracts the complexity of distributed

data systems, enabling data teams to focus on business impact rather than infrastructure plumbing.

Y O U R R O L E

Design and implement scalable, fault-tolerant data pipeline architectures for multi-tenant environments,

mentoring junior engineers on design patterns and tradeoffs (latency vs. throughput, consistency models, cost
optimization)

Lead cross-functional technical initiatives to improve data platform reliability and performance; conduct

architecture reviews and establish coding standards across data engineering teams

Own the evolution of our ETL/ELT orchestration layer (Airflow-based), including scheduler optimization,

DAG patterns, and operational excellence for 500+ production workflows

Drive strategic decisions around data warehouse selection and migration (Snowflake/BigQuery), including cost

analysis, query optimization, and multi-cloud data strategy

Architect and implement Kafka-based event streaming infrastructure for real-time analytics, including schema

management, data quality checks, and disaster recovery protocols

Mentor and develop 4-6 data engineers, providing technical guidance on complex problems, code reviews, and

career development; conduct hiring for engineering team expansion

Establish data modeling standards and governance across the organization, leveraging dbt for transformation

logic and implementing lineage/metadata tracking

 Lead incident response for critical production issues; build observability and alerting systems for data pipelines

using Python-based monitoring frameworks

Collaborate with product and customer success teams on roadmap prioritization and technical feasibility
assessments; represent engineering in quarterly planning cycles

R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

8-12 years of experience building and operating large-scale data infrastructure and pipelines in production

environments

Deep expertise with Apache Spark (RDD/DataFrame APIs, partition strategies, Catalyst optimizer) and

Airflow, with demonstrated ability to design fault-tolerant orchestration systems

Proficiency in Python and SQL, with ability to write efficient, maintainable code; demonstrated expertise

optimizing complex queries for performance and cost

Experience architecting and deploying cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift), including

schema design, cost optimization, and multi-tenant isolation

Proven track record leading technical initiatives and mentoring engineers; experience scaling engineering
teams and establishing engineering culture

Strong systems thinking with understanding of distributed systems concepts (consensus, idempotency, eventual

consistency, data partitioning, sharding)

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Experience with real-time event streaming platforms (Kafka, Pulsar) and stream processing frameworks (Spark

Streaming, Flink)

Hands-on experience with dbt and modern data transformation tools; knowledge of data modeling techniques
(dimensional modeling, star schemas, data vault)

Experience building data quality and observability solutions; familiarity with data catalog and metadata

management tools

Background in fintech, high-frequency trading, or other latency-sensitive domains

Contributions to open-source data projects or strong GitHub presence demonstrating technical depth

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Apache Spark, Airflow, dbt, Snowflake, Python, SQL, Kafka, BigQuery, Kubernetes, Terraform

W E O F F E R

Competitive equity package (0.15-0.35%) with 4-year vesting and 1-year cliff

Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental, vision) with company-paid premiums for employee + family

 Unlimited PTO with minimum 20-day recommendation plus 11 company holidays and parental leave (16

weeks primary, 8 weeks secondary)

Professional development budget ($5,000/year) for conferences, courses, training, and certifications

Home office setup allowance ($2,000) and standing desk/ergonomic equipment stipend

401(k) matching (4% of gross salary) with immediate vesting

Free access to LinkedIn Learning and internal technical mentorship program

Company offsites (2x annual) and team building budget; catered meals and snacks in office

T O A P P LY

Submit your resume, a portfolio or GitHub profile showcasing your most complex data engineering project, and a

cover letter describing your experience with large-scale systems architecture to careers@dataflowsystems.io.

Please also include 1-2 examples of technical decisions you've led that impacted production systems. We review

applications on a rolling basis and will follow up within 1 week.
